title: Fear Garden — Chapter 2: The Garden Toast
description: Chapter 2 of the Fear Garden saga. The case begins with a poisoned beer flight, then widens into inventory fraud tied to the same shadow ledger Carter had started to uncover.

answerKey.method: Swapped Nolan’s marked beer flight with a tainted sample during the garden toast
answerKey.motive: To hide that Trevor had been watering down premium kegs and pocketing the difference
answerKey.keyEvidence: Tap-room pour log, flight marker photo, and deleted keg inventory note
answerKey.explanation: Trevor Knox had the access, timing, and motive. The proof path combines tap-room pour log, flight marker photo, and deleted keg inventory note with the failed statement: Trevor claimed he stayed at the patio gate. The poisoned toast shows that the Root Ledger reaches past donations and into the Beer Garden’s supply chain.
answerKey.sagaConnection: The poisoned toast shows that the Root Ledger reaches past donations and into the Beer Garden’s supply chain.
answerKey.chapterRevealHook: Trevor killed to protect his keg scheme, but the deleted note proves he was not the only person using the ledger. Someone above him was turning small fraud into a business plan.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 1
phone.text: Nolan Graves missed two calls from Trevor Knox before the garden music cut out. This clue belongs to Chapter 2: The Garden Toast.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 2
phone.text: A final call lasted eleven seconds as Nolan moved toward the side corridor.

messages.title: Messages Evidence 1
messages.text: Nolan texted Trevor: “We talk tonight. I saw the numbers.” This clue belongs to Chapter 2: The Garden Toast.

messages.title: Messages Evidence 2
messages.text: Trevor replied: “Not at the bar. Meet by the garden gate.”

openingNarration: Detectives, welcome back to Fear Garden at Mid City Beer Garden in Baton Rouge, Louisiana. This is Chapter 2: The Garden Toast, built for Rookie Detective. Fear Garden is no longer a set of separate cases. It is one continuing story that grows darker and more elaborate with every chapter. After Carter Reed exposed missing charity money, a phrase in his notes—Root Ledger—turns up again in beer inventory records. Tonight's investigation focuses on Nolan Graves. Players now track overlapping money, inventory, and movement clues. The murder is harder, and the story begins connecting staff fraud to a wider expansion scheme. Study every app carefully. Earlier details may return with new meaning, and the Root Ledger thread is now part of the case foundation.
revealScript: Case revealed. Chapter 2: The Garden Toast is closed. The culprit was Trevor Knox. The method: Swapped Nolan’s marked beer flight with a tainted sample during the garden toast. The motive: To hide that Trevor had been watering down premium kegs and pocketing the difference. The proof: Tap-room pour log, flight marker photo, and deleted keg inventory note. Trevor killed to protect his keg scheme, but the deleted note proves he was not the only person using the ledger. Someone above him was turning small fraud into a business plan.

sagaTitle: Fear Garden: The Root Ledger Saga
storyArcSummary: Players now track overlapping money, inventory, and movement clues. The murder is harder, and the story begins connecting staff fraud to a wider expansion scheme.
previousChapterConnection: After Carter Reed exposed missing charity money, a phrase in his notes—Root Ledger—turns up again in beer inventory records.
nextChapterHook: Trevor’s keg fraud was real, but the deleted inventory note mentions a vendor account that does not exist on any official Beer Garden paperwork.
